summary: SALV Phase 2 (encrypted document attachments + partial-release handoffs) backend tested via pytest against the preview URL. 15/16 tests passed; 1 skipped (filesystem ciphertext tamper, because storage backend resolves to S3 in this preview — encrypted-metadata tamper tests cover the same code path and pass). All critical features verified: AES-GCM-256 + HKDF wrap encryption, round-trip integrity with X-NotaryChain-Sha256 header, owner-only writes, beneficiary access gated on released_percent>0, tamper detection (nonce/wrapped_dek), partial-release additive + cap + 400-on-fully-released, Hedera anchor + handoff_token issued, document_count decrements on delete, and private encryption fields (nonce_b64/wrapped_dek_b64/wrap_nonce_b64) are stripped from all public responses.

minor.issue: Spec says 'Third call with percent=999 caps at share_percent (50%)' but PartialReleaseBody enforces Field(gt=0, le=100), so any percent>100 returns 422 BEFORE the cap logic. The capping logic itself works correctly for any 0<percent<=100 that exceeds remaining share (verified: percent=100 on remaining=25 caps to delta=25). Either relax the validator to allow >100 with capping, or update the spec wording. Not a functional defect — caps work — just spec/validator mismatch.

MIME check only triggers if file.content_type is present and not in ALLOWED_MIME_PREFIXES; clients that omit content_type (multipart without explicit type) bypass the allow-list. Low impact since size limit still applies.

action_items: Decide between spec or validator for the >100 percent case on release-partial: either allow large values and let the cap logic clamp, or update spec to say '422 if percent>100; capping happens within the [0,100] range'.
Optional: harden MIME allow-list to also reject when content_type is None (treat missing as disallowed), so the disallowed-MIME path can't be trivially bypassed by clients omitting the header.
critical_code_review_comments: Encryption design is clean: fresh AES-GCM DEK per file + HKDF-SHA256 per-asset wrap key with asset_id salt + AAD bound to asset_id; verified by tamper tests that any byte flip in nonce_b64 or wrapped_dek_b64 in Mongo causes /download to fail with 500 (auth-tag failure).
_public_doc correctly strips nonce_b64/wrapped_dek_b64/wrap_nonce_b64 from all responses; only encryption_alg is exposed. Verified.
_user_allowed_for_asset enforces beneficiary access only when released_percent>0 (Mongo $gt:0 query). Verified end-to-end: alice gets 403 before release, 200 after.
Delete decrements document_count via $inc:-1 atomically; verified post-delete count drops by 1.
SALV_MASTER_KEY falls back to a hardcoded DEV digest with only a warning log. Production deployments must set SALV_MASTER_KEY (and ideally back it with KMS/HSM as the comment notes).

Storage backend is selected at runtime; this preview uses S3 (AWS credentials are configured). The local-FS tamper test was correctly skipped; encryption metadata tamper tests cover the same decrypt-failure code path.
seed_data_creation: Test creates and self-cleans a transient SALV asset + beneficiary for the additive/cap flow. Also uploads + deletes a small text doc and a 26MB rejection probe. Triggers a 1% partial release on alice's existing beneficiary if her released_percent was 0, in order to assert post-release access.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_salv_phase2.py. To run: cd /app/backend && MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=test_database REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<preview-url> python -m pytest tests/test_salv_phase2.py -v. The fs-ciphertext tamper test auto-skips when storage_backend != 'local'. Preview ingress is flaky (occasional 502/504 on Hedera-anchored release calls) — a _post_retry() helper in the file already retries 2x. Hedera anchor presence is asserted only conditionally (best-effort, as designed).
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Two minor design observations documented in backend_issues.minor; both are advisory, not blockers.
